title: Schraderanthus viscosus
taxonomy: Schraderanthus viscosus is a member of the family Brassicaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as mustard or cabbage family. This family is part of the larger clade called Angiosperms, which consists of flowering plants that produce seeds within fruits or flowers. The Brassicaceae family includes many well-known plants such as broccoli, cauliflower, and cabbage, as well as numerous wild species. Schraderanthus viscosus is native to Western Australia and is a shrub-like species with sticky or viscid leaves. Further classification of Schraderanthus viscosus may involve its placement within a specific tribe or genus within the Brassicaceae family.

intro: Schraderanthus viscosus is a plant species native to Australia, commonly known as sticky bush or sticky hop-bush. The plant has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include treating colds, flu, and other respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Schraderanthus viscosus include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve respiratory congestion and soothe sore throats.
